title: The Goal
subtitle: A Process of Ongoing Improvement
author: Eliyahu M. Goldratt and Jeff Cox
themes: The goal of a business is to make money by increasing throughput
Every system has a constraint that limits its performance
Local optima do not produce global optima
Measurements drive behavior — use the right metrics
The Socratic method of learning through guided questions
Continuous improvement is a process, not a destination
Dependent events plus statistical fluctuations produce variability
Balanced plants are not desirable — a bottleneck is necessary
awards: One of the 25 Most Influential Business Management Books (Time Magazine, 2011)
Required reading at Harvard Business School and other top MBA programs
Listed in The Economist's best business books
Jeff Bezos requires Amazon's top management team to read it
excerpt: What is the goal of a business? A plant manager on the brink of closure discovers the answer is not what he was taught — and that finding the bottleneck is the first step to saving everything.
